stories.title: Russia batters Kyiv with massive missile and drone attack
stories.summary: Russia’s military pummeled Kyiv overnight with large-scale drone and missile attack that killed at least two people and injured scores of others, Ukrainian officials said on Sunday. Moscow’s forces used some 600 drones and 30 missiles, including at least one Oreshnik, a powerful hypersonic ballistic missile that is capable of carrying nuclear weapons, Ukraine’s air force said. “The largest number of missiles was directed at the capital — at ordinary residential buildings,

stories.title: Zelenskyy rejects Merz proposal for associate EU membership
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Saturday rejected a German proposal to grant Ukraine associate membership in the EU because it would leave Kyiv without a voice inside the bloc. “There can be no complete European project without Ukraine, and Ukraine’s place in the European Union must also be complete — full and equal ,” Zelenskyy said in a post on X . “It is important to open the clusters” on negotiation areas, he said. “It is important to make meaningful

stories.title: Nationwide pressed to address ‘emerging governance issues’ as AGM looms
stories.summary: Labour MP writes to chair amid concerns building societies are overusing quick votes and failing to add members to boards Nationwide is under pressure to address “emerging governance issues” across the building society sector, amid concerns bosses are bundling voting options and failing to allocate board seats for members. The Stockport Labour MP Navendu Mishra has sent a formal letter to the chair of Nationwide, Kevin Parry, outlining growing unease over the way executives, including at Nationw

stories.title: France bans Israel’s Ben-Gvir from entry
stories.summary: France on Saturday banned Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir from entering French territory following his treatment of activists detained while attempting to break Israel’s naval blockade of Gaza. “As of this day, Itamar Ben-Gvir is banned from accessing French territory,” Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot announced in a social media post. “This decision follows his unacceptable actions toward French and European citizens aboard the Global Sumud flotilla .

stories.title: A magyar gazdasági fordulat titkos fegyvere: a jól működő versenypolitika
stories.summary: A NER évei alatt a politika szisztematikusan pusztította a piaci versenyt, ami nagyban hozzájárult ahhoz, hogy lemaradjon a magyar gazdaság. Most itt a lehetőség, hogy újból legyen a cégek közötti egészséges versengés, aminek sok pozitív hozadéka lenne.

stories.title: David Szalay: Test (részlet)
stories.summary: A tizenöt éves félénk és esetlen István édesanyjával él egy csendes magyarországi lakótelepen. Új fiú a városban, aki hamarosan elszigetelődik az osztálytársaitól, és olyan eseményekbe sodródik, amelyek örökre idegenné teszik önmaga számára. Egyedüli társasága a szomszédban élő, férjezett nő, aki nagyjából egyidős az édesanyjával. Kapcsolatuk titkos viszonnyá alakul, amelynek igazi jelentőségét István maga sem fogja fel teljesen, és végül az élete kisiklik az irányítása alól. David Szalay 2025-b

stories.title: Magyar Péter az államtitkárokról: Egyharmaduk nő, és összesen 103 diplomájuk van
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök szerint a jogi, közgazdasági, műszaki-informatikai és társadalomtudományi háttér a leggyakoribb a kinevezett államtitkárok között.

stories.title: Tóth Gábor korábbi budapesti rendőrfőkapitány lesz a rendészeti államtitkár
stories.summary: Tóth 2007-től, a második Gyurcsány-kormány és a Bajnai-kormány alatt volt a főváros rendőrfőkapitánya, majd 2010-ben leváltották.

stories.title: Magaslatok és áramlatok – élő zenei élmény felsőfokon (x)
stories.summary: A Liszt Ferenc Kamarazenekar bemutatta következő szezonjának budapesti koncertjeit.

stories.title: Nagy Ervin kultúráért felelős államtitkár: A kultúra nem eladó, nem elidegeníthető
stories.summary: Hivatalossá vált, milyen kormányzati pozíciót kap Nagy Ervin. A színész azt írta, arra vállalkozott, hogy a legjobb tudása szerint szolgálja a magyar kulturális életet.

stories.title: Orbán Anita a Kijev elleni brutális orosz támadásról: Határozottan elítéljük
stories.summary: A külügyminiszter szerint a civileknek soha nem szabadna rakétákra, drónokra és robbanásokra ébredniük.

stories.title: „Ide figyelj, te otromba, sovén Tanasă!” – Egymásnak ment az RMDSZ és a román szélsőjobb a felújított Rákóczi-vár miatt

stories.summary: Az esztergom-budapesti érsek legutóbb február 2-án tartott nyilvános szentmisét.

stories.title: Az Egyház jövőjénél most a hitelesség kérdése a kulcs – a pannonhalmi főapát egy új korszakról
stories.summary: A magyar kereszténység társadalmi, politikai, kulturális értelemben is új korszakhoz ért: ebben pedig nem az lesz a kérdés, hogyan tudja társadalmi súlyát képviselni, hanem az, hogy mennyire lesz hiteles. Hortobágyi T. Cirill írása pünkösdre.

stories.title: French stars are rightly worried by billionaire Vincent Bolloré. Here’s how to rein him in
stories.summary: The conservative tycoon’s grip on media and cinema is unhealthy. An EU fund could protect democracy in perpetuity The shadow of Joseph McCarthy’s “red scare” loomed over the storied steps of this year’s Cannes film festival. Echoing the mid-20th-century blacklist, which shut out about 300 suspected communists from Hollywood, the French media group Canal+ announced an effective ban on twice that many French cinema professionals, including actors such as Juliette Binoche and film directors such as

stories.title: In France, pro-Palestinian solidarity is being silenced and criminalised | Rokhaya Diallo
stories.summary: The government’s deeply illiberal ‘Yadan bill’ may be dead. But the threat to legitimate protest certainly is not Tensions in France over how to respond to a rise in antisemitism have been running high. A government-backed bill that aimed to deal with the problem was rightly denounced as an attack on freedom of expression before being quietly shelved by the government last month. Introduced in 2024 by Caroline Yadan , a member of the national assembly, the draft legislation was intended to count

stories.title: US judge dismisses criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García
stories.summary: Judge said Trump administration would not have prosecuted Ábrego had he not challenged his high-profile deportation Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email A US judge dismissed a criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García on Friday, finding that the Trump administration would not have prosecuted him had he not challenged his high-profile deportation. Ábrego, who had entered the United States without authorization in the past, became a symbol of the Trump administration’s drive fo

stories.title: Gaudi-Nagy Tamás: Büntetőjogi és polgári jogi elégtételt fogok venni Magyar Péteren
stories.summary: Az ügyvéd szerint a miniszterelnök „kényszerítő és besározó szándékkal” járt el, amikor nyilvánosan összefüggésbe hozta őt a kegyelmi üggyel. Felvette a kapcsolatot Schiffer Andrással.

stories.summary: A tüntetés nagyrészt békésen zajlott, de a rendőrség szerint egy kisebb csoport megpróbálta áttörni a Sánchez rezidenciáját védő kordont a Moncloa-palotánál.

stories.summary: Augusztus 20-ig befejeződhetnek az átvilágítások, csökkenteni fogják a kormányfő mellett az állami vezetők, képviselők és a polgármesterek fizetését is. Az RTL-nek adott első kormányfői nagyinterjújában azt mondta, a hétvégén kihirdetik az összes államtitkárt.

stories.summary: Bük város önkormányzati képviselője kikérte az adatokat, de felhívta rá a figyelmet: ez még nem jelenti azt, hogy a településen fel is használták az anyagokat.

stories.summary: Az egy győzelemmel és négy vereséggel álló magyarok legközelebb hétfőn találkoznak a címvédő amerikaiakkal.

stories.title: Mága Zoltán vendégénekese több pénzt kapott az NKA-tól, mint a teljes magyar folyóiratpiac
stories.summary: Ráadásul egyelőre még nem jutottak hozzá az idei pénzükhöz, ezért megkongatták a vészharangot az alulfinanszírozott, végveszélybe került lapok, amelyek az éves működésük finanszírozására pályázhattak az NKA-hoz.

stories.title: Viola Beáta: Kényszerfék
stories.summary: „Milyen gyakran találkozunk? Nyári szezonban kéthetente jár a Budapesthez közeli vonalakon. Télen egyszer-kétszer meglátogatom.” E heti novellánk középpontjában egy különleges kapcsolat áll.

stories.title: Balog Zoltán szerint csak K. Endre felesége kért tőle segítséget a kegyelmi ügyben, senki más
stories.summary: Az Orbán-kormány egykori emberminisztere szerint egyedül ő járt közben az államfőnél, és csak a politikai propaganda akarja a Fidesz-kormányt, Orbán Viktort és családját belekeverni a kegyelmi botrányba.

stories.title: Czechia: Uproar over Sudeten German gathering in Brno
stories.summary: The first gathering of Sudeten Germans in Czechia since World War II is happening this weekend, despite a political backlash that has exposed tensions over one of Central Europe's most painful historical legacies.

stories.title: Latvia scrambles NATO jets in drone alert, the latest of several in Baltic
stories.summary: Latvia's military said NATO planes responded to at least one drone flying in its airspace. It's the latest in a string of such incidents in the Baltic, one of which eventually toppled Latvia's government.

stories.title: Hungary's new Prime Minister Magyar seeks to fix Poland ties
stories.summary: Peter Magyar hopes to mend relations with Poland during his first state visit to Warsaw. What lessons could he learn from Poland's Prime Minister Donald Tusk?

stories.title: What to know about the AI models that are jolting Washington
stories.summary: Anthropic and OpenAI have spent the last month touting the hacking capabilities of their new artificial intelligence models. Researchers with access to these tools say they’re not exaggerating — and warn that the fallout could be even larger than imagined, as tools such as Anthropic’s Claude Mythos and OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 continue to develop. “It was very clear to me that this was going to be a game-changer,” Lee Klarich, chief product and technology officer at cybersecurity company Palo Alto Netwo

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy sounds warning on Oreshnik missiles as Russia strikes Kyiv
stories.summary: Ukrainian president says any use of such weapons ‘sets a global precedent for other potential aggressors’ as blasts heard throughout the capital. What we know on day 1,551 Continue reading...

stories.title: Russia hits Kyiv with hypersonic ballistic missile in ‘deranged attack’
stories.summary: At least four people died and dozens of others were injured in the attack Russia used its powerful hypersonic Oreshnik ballistic missile for a third time in Ukraine as part of a massive attack on Kyiv and its surrounding region that killed at least four people and injured dozens. Russia hit the city of Bila Tserkva in the Kyiv region with the missile, Ukraine’s president Volodymyr Zelenskyy said. He described a Russian assault that hit a water supply facility, burnt down a market, damaged dozens

The Directorate General of Civil Defence said the building had collapsed and a large number of vehicles and equipm

stories.title: War-driven gas spike in US puts pressure on summer travelers — and Republicans
stories.summary: WINSTON-SALEM, North Carolina — The spike in gas prices is a national political calamity for the White House — but it’s also a local political challenge in every state of the union as Americans hit the road for the busiest travel season of the year. While the Trump administration is  focused on $5.02  — the per-gallon high-water mark hit under the Biden administration in 2022 as Russia’s invasion of Ukraine snarled energy supplies — gas prices in many states are nearing or have already

stories.title: Serbia: Police clash with protesters urging early elections
stories.summary: Officers fired teargas and stun grenades as tens of thousands rallied against President Aleksandar Vucic's government. The demonstration went ahead despite the state rail operator canceling trains to and from Belgrade.

stories.title: Trump says peace deal with Iran is imminent
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump on Saturday announced what he described as a near-finalized agreement among the U.S., Iran and several Middle Eastern intermediaries aimed at ending a monthslong war that has destabilized global shipping routes, rattled energy markets and fueled fears of broader economic fallout. In a post on Truth Social, Trump said he had convened a call from the Oval Office with leaders from Middle Eastern countries, including Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates, writing, “An

stories.title: Former prosecutor calls for EU statute blocking US sanctions on ICC members
stories.summary: US imposed sanctions on nine judges and a prosecutor after ICC issued arrest warrants for members of Israeli cabinet A former prosecutor at the international criminal court has called for an EU-wide statute blocking what she describes as “thuggish” and “bullying” US sanctions imposed on members of the court that are designed to send the court into oblivion. In February 2025, the US imposed sanctions on 11 ICC officials, including nine judges and the chief prosecutor as well three Palestinian org

stories.title: Irish gangland figure fails in Dublin byelection bid for seat in parliament
stories.summary: Gerry ‘the monk’ Hutch comes fourth in contest won by Daniel Ennis of Social Democrats The Irish gangland figure Gerry “the monk” Hutch has failed in his bid for a parliamentary seat in a Dublin byelection. The 63-year-old came fourth in a contest won by Daniel Ennis of the Social Democrats, a victory for progressive politics after a campaign dominated by concerns over the cost of living and immigration. Continue reading...

stories.title: Former prosecutor pursued by Trump calls for crackdown on election lies: ‘Lying can be held to account’
stories.summary: Andrew Weissmann argues for new law to hold political liars like US president accountable for harming democracy Politicians must be held accountable if their lies damage democracy, according to a former US federal prosecutor and FBI general counsel who was pursued by Donald Trump . The US must be “as creative as possible” and introduce sweeping structural reforms if it escapes its current “mess”, said Andrew Weissmann, laying out a proposal for a legislative crackdown on election deceit. stories.title: UK judge’s decision not to jail boys for rape like a ‘rock in my face’, says victim, 16
stories.summary: Boys, aged 15, given youth rehabilitation orders for two separate attacks against two girls in Hampshire A judge’s decision not to jail the teenage boys who raped two girls has been described as a “rock straight in my face” by one of their victims. Southampton crown court heard the two boys, both aged 15 at the time, raped the teenage girls in two separate attacks that occurred on 26 November 2024 and 17 January 2025 in Fordingbridge, Hampshire. Continue reading...

stories.title: US voters support HIV/Aids relief – will Trump’s cuts backfire in the midterms?
stories.summary: Global Pepfar program has long had Republican leadership and bipartisan support, but initiative is under fire US midterm voters overwhelmingly support Pepfar, an initiative to end HIV/Aids that also has strengthened health systems against other infectious disease threats but has come under fire from the Trump administration . About three in four (74%) of likely voters in the US midterm elections say they support funding the US President’s Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief (Pepfar), with voters more

stories.title: Extra EU border checks suspended at Dover as travellers face delays in heat
stories.summary: Wait times of more than two hours reported at terminal for cross-Channel ferry to France French police have temporarily suspended extra EU border checks at the port of Dover as thousands of holidaymakers face long delays in the hot weather. Waiting times of more than two hours were reported at the terminal in Kent for the cross-Channel ferry to France. Continue reading...

stories.title: US: Suspect dies after gunfire near White House
stories.summary: There was a massive police response after gunshots were fired near the White House, where President Donald Trump was working on a deal with Iran. The suspect has been shot and killed by law enforcement.

stories.title: Cyprus votes amid resentment over corruption, living costs
stories.summary: Cyprus has begun voting in a parliamentary election that could shift the political balance amid public discontent.

stories.title: Thousands rally in Madrid to demand Sanchez's resignation
stories.summary: Mounting corruption allegations involving Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez’s family, allies and party sparked the protest.

stories.title: I survived a missile strike in the Strait of Hormuz, but my friend has not been found
stories.summary: Sunil Puniya was on his first sea job when a missile struck the oil tanker Skylight at the start of the Iran war.

stories.summary: Japanese conglomerate SoftBank Group recently donated $50 million to the Trump presidential library and foundation, two people familiar with the matter said, as companies continue to curry favor with the Trump administration. The donation from SoftBank is one of the largest known gifts to the fund. stories.title: ‘There is profound disappointment in him’: mood in Russia turns against Putin
stories.summary: Increasingly isolated president is determined to press on with Ukraine war, say well-placed sources, despite ailing economy Vladimir Putin pulled up to a hotel in central Moscow earlier in May in a Russian-made SUV, dressed casually in jeans and a light jacket. Carrying a bouquet of flowers, he walked unhurriedly into the lobby and embraced his former schoolteacher Vera Gurevich, who kissed him on both cheeks. He then helped Gurevich into his car and drove her to dinner at the Kremlin. Continue

stories.title: Shark attack: man dies on Great Barrier Reef in far north Queensland
stories.summary: The 39-year-old was reportedly fishing at Kennedy Shoal between Cairns and Townsville when emergency services alerted about midday Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A man has died after a shark attack on the Great Barrier Reef south of Cairns. The 39-year-old had reportedly been fishing at Kennedy Shoal, a shallow reef about 50km off the Queensland coast, between Cairns and Townsville. Continue reading...

stories.title: Tour groups, temporary routes and toilets: the reshaping of Rome – photo essay
stories.summary: Photographer Lorenzo Grifantini looks at how the Italian capital’s historic centre has gradually reorganised itself around the uninterrupted flow of visitors and the expectations projected on to it By mid-morning, the area around the Trevi fountain is already difficult to cross. Visitors stop suddenly to take photographs while tour groups gather behind raised umbrellas, and security staff redirect the flow of people through temporary barriers placed around the monument. stories.title: Hunger increasingly used as weapon of war as ‘food-related violence’ surges, analysis shows
stories.summary: More than 20,000 attacks on markets, farmland and food distribution systems have been recorded since 2018 Hunger is being increasingly exploited as a weapon of war with more than 20,000 documented incidents of “food-related violence” in the past eight years, new analysis reveals. Attacks include 1,261 strikes on markets used by families for daily groceries and 863 incidents in which food distribution systems were targeted and workers killed. Continue reading...

stories.title: What may be included in proposed Iran ceasefire deal?

stories.title: Iran’s leader and security council ‘still need to approve’ peace deal with US
stories.summary: One or two clauses yet to be clarified, Iran officials tell Pakistani mediators, after US says deal ‘largely negotiated’ Middle East crisis: live updates Iran’s supreme leader and national security council still need to approve the proposed peace deal between Tehran and Washington, Iranian officials said on Sunday. stories.title: Ohio State reels from multiple scandals amid wider crisis in higher education

stories.title: Andy Burnham seeks advice from Sue Gray on forming future Labour government
stories.summary: Discussions highlight how seriously senior Labour figures are treating Burnham’s path back to Westminster Andy Burnham has sought advice from Sue Gray, Keir Starmer’s former chief of staff, on how to manage a potential transition into Downing Street if he returns to Westminster and succeeds the prime minister. Lady Gray is understood to have advised Burnham on how a future government could be formed as Labour’s internal succession chatter intensifies before the Makerfield byelection. Continue re

stories.title: UK heatwave expected as temperatures near record highs for May
stories.summary: Forecasters say temperatures could hit 34C on Monday, with amber health alerts in place across much of England A heatwave is expected to be declared in parts of the UK on Sunday, with temperatures nearing May records. The Met Office said readings at Heathrow at midday on Sunday showed temperatures had reached 28C (82F) across three consecutive days – the threshold for declaring a heatwave. Continue reading...

stories.title: River Wye formally recognised as living ecosystem with intrinsic rights
stories.summary: Charter to be adopted along river’s entire catchment from Cambrian mountains to Chepstow and Bristol Channel The entire catchment of the River Wye has been formally recognised as a living ecosystem with intrinsic rights in a charter, a UK first that campaigners hope will help save the highly polluted river. The charter was celebrated at a community event at the Hay-on-Wye literary festival on Sunday. It includes the right to flow, to biodiversity, to be free from pollution, to be supported by a

stories.title: White House pauses removal of detainees to DRC as Ebola outbreak widens
stories.summary: But Trump administration will not return detainees deported to third countries in disease-stricken region The Trump administration will temporarily pause the removal of refugees to the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) during a spiraling Ebola outbreak, according to reporting by Politico, but experts say the move won’t help prevent the spread of the disease. At least one woman is now in limbo after officials moved her to Kinshasa, the capital of the DRC, and now say they won’t bring her bac

stories.title: China mine death toll at least 82 after gas blast
stories.summary: Xi Jinping urges authorities to ‘spare no effort’ in rescue operation after explosion in northern Shanxi province At least 82 people have been killed in a gas explosion at a coalmine in northern China’s Shanxi province, in the country’s worst mining disaster in 17 years. The explosion happened on Friday at 7.29pm (12.29 BST) while 247 workers were underground at the Liushenyu coalmine in Qinyuan county, the state media agency Xinhua reported. Continue reading...

stories.title: Iran war: Donald Trump says deal 'largely negotiated'
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Rubio has said an announcement on the deal could come as early as Sunday. Pakistan's top diplomat has cited "meaningful progress," while Israel's Netanyahu reportedly voiced concern. Follow DW.

stories.title: Displaced Ukrainians risk home seizure under Russian law
stories.summary: Russia is tightening control over property in occupied Ukrainian territories, requiring owners to register under Russian law. Many Ukrainians say returning to do so would put their safety and freedom at risk.

stories.title: Australia: Man dies of shark attack on eastern coast
stories.summary: The man was bitten by a shark at Kennedy Shoal in Australia's northeastern state of Queensland, in the second shark attack in the country this month.

stories.title: Philippines: 20 feared trapped after building collapse
stories.summary: The building collapsed before dawn after a severe thunderstorm. Officials said rescuers reported "hearing voices" in the rubble.

stories.title: Deadly blast hits train in Pakistan's Balochistan
stories.summary: A blast on a train in Pakistan's Balochistan province has killed at least 24 people and injured dozens more. The explosion targeted military personnel and their families ahead of the Eid holiday.

stories.title: Russia uses hypersonic Oreshnik missile in Kyiv attack
stories.summary: Russia carried out a massive drone and missile attack on the capital, Kyiv, and its surrounding region overnight, killing at least four people. France and Germany have condemned Moscow's use of the Oreshnik missile.

stories.title: New Chinese surveillance leaves foreigners nowhere to hide
stories.summary: A Chinese cybersecurity expert has revealed to DW details of China's new high-tech policing. From ski resort facial recognition to seats on a train, the system can track anyone and compile a "holistic profile."

stories.title: US, Iran strike upbeat tone as Hormuz deal takes shape
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump flagged progress in talks with Iran, Tehran has warned that major gaps still stand in the way of dialing down the conflict.

stories.title: Trump says Iran deal 'largely negotiated' including reopening Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Tehran also signals progress on talks but says the key issue of nuclear weapons is not part of an initial framework it is working on.

stories.title: At least 82 killed in Chinese coal mine explosion
stories.summary: The blast in Shanxi province constitutes the country's worst mining disaster in 16 years.

stories.title: It's like the Olympics - except steroids are allowed
stories.summary: The first ever Enhanced Games are taking place this weekend in Las Vegas, with big names, big money and much controversy.

stories.title: Gunman killed after shootout with Secret Service near White House, Trump says
stories.summary: Trump said the shooter had a "violent history and possible obsession" with the White House.

stories.title: Heavy police presence outside White House after deadly shooting at security checkpoint
stories.summary: Secret Service officers and police sealed off streets around the White House after a person opened fire at a security checkpoint before being shot dead by officers, according to US authorities.

stories.title: Suspect killed after opening fire on Secret Service near White House
stories.summary: Officials say a suspect approached a checkpoint near the building and opened fire, and that a bystander was wounded.

stories.title: Fighter jet tracks UFO in newly released Pentagon footage
stories.summary: The Pentagon released another batch of previously classified UFO files, including military videos of unidentified aerial phenomena, saying the material is intended to provide “unprecedented transparency” despite offering no evidence of alien life.

stories.title: Man killed in shark attack off Australia's north-east coast
stories.summary: The 39-year-old was fatally injured while swimming out at a shoal off the Cassowary Coast in Queensland.

stories.title: Pakistan train bombing kills at least 24 people
stories.summary: Pakistan's prime minister, Shehbaz Sharif, said he strongly condemned the "heinous bomb explosion" and sent his condolences to the victims' families.

stories.title: BBC at the site of China's worst mining disaster in more than a decade
stories.summary: At least 82 people have been killed after a gas explosion at the Liushenyu mine in China.

stories.title: Red Cross volunteers die from suspected Ebola in DR Congo
stories.summary: They are thought to have caught the virus before the outbreak was identified, the Red Cross says.

stories.title: Bolivian minister's convoy ambushed while overseeing roadblock clearance
stories.summary: Security forces were met by resistance while attempting to clear highway barriers, amid unrest over the government's economic policies.

stories.title: People trapped under collapsed building in Philippines
stories.summary: A search operation has so far rescued 24 people with no deaths reported at the site, in a city near the capital Manila.

stories.title: Putin vows retaliation after accusing Ukraine of hitting student dormitory
stories.summary: Ukraine says it struck Russia's elite Rubicon drone military unit in a Moscow-occupied region.

stories.title: French mum and partner held until trial after sons abandoned by road in Portugal
stories.summary: The couple, who allegedly left the woman's sons on a roadside in southern Portugal, will remain in custody.

stories.title: F1 great Prost 'injured as masked gang raid home'
stories.summary: Four-time Formula 1 world champion Alain Prost was injured when a masked gang raided his home near Geneva, Swiss media reports.

stories.title: Denmark’s Frederiksen gets a second chance to form next government
stories.summary: Danish caretaker Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen announced a new round of talks to form the next government would begin Sunday after she was reappointed by the king to lead the coalition negotiations. Denmark’s royal household on Saturday issued a statement saying that King Frederik X “requested acting Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen to lead negotiations on forming a government.” Frederiksen failed to form a new coalition in the first round of negotiations before handing over to De

stories.title: This is how to defeat Vladimir Putin | Timothy Garton Ash
stories.summary: The Russian dictator’s dreams of greatness threaten Nato and the EU, not just Ukraine. Here are eight ways in which he can be thwarted No dictator lasts for ever. One day Vladimir Putin will be gone. Recent reports suggest growing weakness in the Russian economy , discontent in society and a waning of confidence inside his regime – but it would be foolish to conclude the end is near. Only death or Russia can depose Putin, and nobody knows when or how that will happen. What democracies in Europe

stories.title: India’s government orders handover of exclusive Delhi Gymkhana Club
stories.summary: Move comes against backdrop of challenge by prime minister, Narendra Modi, to country’s traditional elites The Indian government has ordered one of the country’s most exclusive private clubs to vacate its premises in two weeks, underscoring the long-running push of the prime minister, Narendra Modi, against entrenched elite institutions. The ministry of housing and urban affairs directed the Delhi Gymkhana Club to hand over its sprawling site in the heart of New Delhi by 5 June, citing urgent pu

stories.title: Pakistan's army chief arrives in Tehran as Rubio signals 'slight progress' in peace talks
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has described the stop-start negotiations this week as teetering on the "borderline" between a deal and renewed attacks.

stories.title: ‘Every health facility said they were full’: alarm over rapid spread of Ebola in DRC
stories.summary: New strain of virus, aid cuts, and cultural norms around burials and touch add to difficulties in stemming outbreak The warnings from aid groups and healthcare workers in the Democratic Republic of the Congo have been stark, their calls for coordinated international action impassioned. As the country reels from the return of the Ebola virus, there is growing concern that its fragile healthcare system will struggle to cope with an outbreak that experts say goes well beyond the number of confirmed

stories.title: Stephen Colbert hosts Michigan public access show day after last appearance on Late Show
stories.summary: Guests on Only in Monroe included Jack White and Jeff Daniels, with appearances by Steve Buscemi and Eminem After his last appearance on CBS as host of The Late Show, Stephen Colbert continued his television hosting duties – on a public access show in Michigan . Colbert on Friday night hosted Only in Monroe in Monroe, Michigan, just one day after signing off from The Late Show. Colbert had been the host of The Late Show since 2015 and his tenure came to an end this week after CBS canceled t

stories.title: No refunds for 15,000 Australian ticket holders after Candace Owens’ tour cancelled
stories.summary: Rightwing commentator says she is hundreds of thousands of dollars out of pocket and claims she was misled by promoter Rocksman Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast None of the 15,000 ticket holders for conservative influencer Candace Owens’ cancelled Australian tour are expected to get their money back from the promoter, after it spent all its money then collapsed. Owens herself says she is hundreds of thousands of dollars out of pocket and claims she was misled by the p

stories.title: France bans Israel minister Ben-Gvir after flotilla video
stories.summary: The Israeli far-right minister released a video taunting detained Gaza aid flotilla activists, prompting widespread criticism at home and internationally.

stories.title: In Iran, internet access becomes a luxury for the few
stories.summary: Despite the ongoing internet blockade in Iran, access to "Internet-Pro" is available to select groups for a fee. Access to information and communication is increasingly becoming a luxury.

stories.title: Couple remanded in custody after boys found abandoned by road in Portugal
stories.summary: A French woman is accused of abandoning her children in Portugal after reportedly blindfolding them and leading them into a wooded area during a game. Her French partner also faces charges.

stories.title: Cheap attack drones break through Israel's Iron Dome
stories.summary: Inexpensive fiber-optic drones are challenging Israel's high-tech defenses, shifting the military balance in the Middle East.

stories.title: Cyprus election expected to shake up national politics
stories.summary: Cyprus goes to the polls to elect a new parliament on Sunday. The vote is expected to reshape the country's political landscape, weaken traditional parties and accelerate the rise of new anti-establishment movements.

stories.title: Kane's hat trick seals Bayern Munich's 21st German Cup win
stories.summary: Harry Kane scored three times to fire Bayern Munich to a 3-0 victory over Stuttgart in the German Cup final. The win delivered a domestic double for the Bavarian giants and ended a six-year trophy drought.

stories.title: Watch: Pitches, PlayStations and protein ice cream - A look inside Brazil's World Cup base camp
stories.summary: BBC's Brandon Livesay toured the Columbia Park Training Facility in Morris Township, New Jersey that will host the team in the coming weeks.

stories.title: Inside Germany's beer obsession, from a 5,000-metre beer pipeline to home-brewing championships
stories.summary: Germany is well-known for its obsession with beer, with the Oktoberfest festival attracting millions of visitors each year.

stories.title: India's parody 'cockroach party' claims website has been blocked
stories.summary: The satirical group has gained millions of followers as "the voice of the lazy and unemployed".

stories.title: US and Iran 'getting a lot closer' to an agreement to end war, Trump says
stories.summary: The US and Iran are close to signing a Pakistan-drafted framework agreement to end the war, with a decision expected within 48 hours, according to officials who spoke in anonymity. The proposal sets a 30-to-60-day window to negotiate details, though nuclear issues are excluded.

stories.title: Nascar champion Kyle Busch died of pneumonia and sepsis, family says
stories.summary: The driver died aged 41 on Thursday after "overwhelming complications" from the illnesses.

stories.title: California declares emergency as fire crews race to contain toxic leak
stories.summary: Thousands have been evacuated as officials warn a tank containing about 7,000 gallons of methyl methacrylate could explode.

stories.title: SpaceX Starship explodes in Indian Ocean after splashdown
stories.summary: SpaceX launched its latest Starship test flight from Texas, with the spacecraft completing most mission objectives before exploding in the Indian Ocean after splashdown.

stories.title: Rubio hints at 'good news' on Iran deal after Trump suggests agreement is imminent
stories.summary: On Sunday, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio told reporters in India there was a chance of some "some good news" regarding a deal later in the day.

stories.title: Rosenberg: Luhansk strike sparks Russian accusations and vow to retaliate
stories.summary: Eighteen people were killed and 42 others injured in the strike on the town in Russian-occupied eastern Ukraine.

stories.title: In Japan, divorce splits parents from children. Could a law change end sole custody?
stories.summary: An amendment to Japan's Civil Code looks set to allow shared parenting for the first time.

stories.title: East Africa wants to curb imports of used clothes. But it's not easy
stories.summary: Kenya, Uganda and Tanzania are major importers of used garments from the West and China.

stories.title: Teszt előtt a hazai napelemes és az egész energiapiac, leomlanak-e a láthatatlan gátak?
stories.summary: Ha idehaza nem érvényesülnek az energiamamutokra vonatkozó szétválasztási szabályok, akkor a napelemet, energiatárolót telepítő háztartások és cégek fizetik meg az összejátszás árát.

stories.title: Közel ezermilliárd forint jó eséllyel menthető a NER-es magántőkealapokból, a többi rázósabb lesz
stories.summary: Összesen több mint 2600 milliárd forintra vállalt kötelezettséget az állam az előző kormány alatt magántőkealapok felé. Mint kiderült, ebből még nem folyósítottak mindent, ami jó alap a vagyonvisszaszerzéshez. Az viszont káros lenne, ha mindenhol azonnal kihúznák a dugót.

stories.title: Lövöldözés történt a Fehér Ház környékén, a támadó meghalt
stories.summary: Donald Trump is a Fehér Házban tartózkodott a lövöldözés idején, de egyelőre nem kommentálta a történteket.

stories.title: Sakkfeladvány: Csak óvatosan, a nagy túlerő is veszélyes
stories.summary: A kegyelemdöfés bevitele egyszerűnek tűnik ebben az állásban, mégsem az.

stories.title: Tüntetők csaptak össze a rendőrökkel Belgrádban, 23 embert őrizetbe vettek
stories.summary: A több tízezres, békésen lezajló kormányellenes tüntetés után maszkos fiatalok blokádokat állítottak fel, és a rendőrséggel is összecsaptak.

stories.title: Gisèle Pelicot újra rátalált a szerelemre
stories.summary: Pelicot, akit a volt férje tíz éven keresztül nagy dózisú nyugtatóval elkábított, azt mondta, soha nem gondolta volna, hogy újra képes lesz megbízni egy férfiban, mégis ez történt vele.

stories.title: Szájkarate elődöntő: Az aranykonvoj vs. Trashcelebek
stories.summary: Keressük a választási kampány legbizarrabb elemét. Itt már a döntőbe megy a győztes, de vajon melyik fog? Szavazzon!

stories.title: Nicolas Cage Pókembere nem fogja vissza magát
stories.summary: Sötét Pókember-sztori, Frankenstein megtalálja a menyasszonyát, és jó kislányok is a heti streamingajánlónkban.

stories.title: Több száz drónnal és több tucat rakétával bombázták az oroszok Kijevet, minden kerületben keletkezett kár
stories.summary: Zelenszkij szerint Oroszország Oresnyik rakétával támadta meg a kijevi régiót.

stories.title: Léteznek még elfogadható árú kisautók, csak jó helyen kell keresni őket
stories.summary: Egy kézi váltós Seatot hasonlítottunk össze egy lágyhibrid-segédhajtású Hyundaijal és egy teljes értékű hibrid Toyotával. Jelentősek a különbségek, és nemcsak az autók fogyasztásában, de a menetdinamikájukban is.

stories.title: Bodor Ádám lekottázva: a 90 éves szerző művéből írt kamaraoperát mutatták be Kolozsváron
stories.summary: A Nikita a Verhovina madarai egyik fejezetéből indult ki, de nem adaptáció: Márkos Albert zeneszerző és Porogi Dorka rendező olyan önálló művet hozott létre, amelyben Bodor különös, hófödte határvidéke a zene és a színház nyelvén szólal meg.

stories.title: Heidl György eszmetörténész társadalmi kapcsolatokért felelős államtitkár lesz
stories.summary: A pécsi egyetem oktatója felel majd a nemzetpolitikáért, illetve a civil, a nemzetiségi és a felekezeti ügyekért.

stories.title: A Fidesz szerint törvényesen és átláthatóság biztosításával hajtották végre a központi költségvetést
stories.summary: Aki mást állít, hazudik, közölte az ellenzéki párt.

stories.title: XIV. Leó: A háborút nem egy szuperhatalom nyeri meg, hanem a mindenható szeretet
stories.summary: Az egyházfő a Szent Péter-bazilikában tartott homíliájában arról beszélt, az emberiséget a nyomortól nem a megszámolhatatlan gazdagság, hanem a szeretet kifogyhatatlan ajándéka szabadítja meg.

stories.title: A második világháború óta először keletkezett kár az ukrán külügyminisztérium épületében
stories.summary: A közelben történt robbanások miatt törtek be az épület ablakai.

stories.title: PM says Alberta ‘essential’ to Canada as separatists push for independence
stories.summary: Alberta premier calls for referendum on secession after judge ruled initiative to force binding vote invalid The Canadian prime minister, Mark Carney , has said that Alberta is “essential” to the country’s future, hours after the province’s leader moved the oil-rich region closer toward a referendum on independence. Separatists in the western province spent months collecting signatures seeking to trigger a binding October vote on seceding from the nation. Continue reading...

stories.title: Jacinta Allan launches state-sponsored apprenticeships at Victoria’s Labor conference ahead of election
stories.summary: The premier is offering 2,000 apprenticeships at the revived State Electricity Commission as part of a pre-election push Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Jacinta Allan has used Labor’s final state conference before the election to announce a plan for a government-owned electricity body to hire apprentices to address significant workforce shortages and job insecurity. The Victorian premier announced the plan in a speech that drew on her father’s experience as a linesma

stories.title: Albanese joins coalition of nations calling for an end to Israeli settlement expansion in the West Bank
stories.summary: Australia joins the UK, Italy, France, Germany, Canada, Norway, the Netherlands and New Zealand in condemning illegal settlements Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Israeli government is undermining stability in the West Bank as settler violence reaches unprecedented levels, a coalition of western countries says, as its leaders call for an end to construction of Israeli settlements it says breach international law. In a joint statement issued on Friday, Anthony Alba

stories.title: UK needs ‘national consensus’ over rejoining EU, David Miliband says
stories.summary: Ex-foreign secretary urges reset at ‘higher dosage’ after officials revealed to have pitched single market for goods Britain needs a “national consensus” about rejoining the European Union, David Miliband has said, in response to revelations that the UK government pitched the creation of a single market for goods with the EU to the bloc. The former foreign secretary, who is now president of the International Rescue Committee, said he thought the UK needed a reset of its relations with the EU at

stories.title: ‘Canada is handing people over to ICE’: refugees rejected at border face US detention
stories.summary: As Canada tightens asylum rules, refugees reuniting with family say they were turned over to ICE and jailed for months after failed border claims As each day in US detention passes, Markens Appolon can feel the life he had dreamed of slipping away. The 25-year-old fled Haiti to escape the rampant gang violence that upended his university studies in economics, and planned to join family in Montreal. Continue reading...

stories.title: Former F1 world champion Alain Prost reportedly injured in robbery at Swiss home
stories.summary: Police investigating an alleged home invasion 71-year-old sustained a head injury, says reports Swiss police are investigating an alleged robbery with reports that the four-times Formula 1 world champion Alain Prost was injured during a home invasion. The Swiss tabloid Blick reported late on Friday that the 71-year-old Frenchman sustained a head injury from intruders who forced his son to open a safe during the incident on Tuesday morning. “The perpetrators entered the residence while the occupa

stories.title: Côte d’Ivoire wary of jihadist threat in north 10 years on from major attack
stories.summary: Threat of jihadism continues to lurk along borders with Mali and Burkina Faso These days, when she is not organising the annual International Day of Reggae celebrations in Côte d’Ivoire, Rose Ebirim picks up litter scattered on the beach in the historic port town of Grand Bassam, 25 miles east of Abidjan. Both activities have become a form of therapy since the time she saw someone die. “13 March 2016 was a Black Sunday for me,” she said. Continue reading...

stories.title: US or Iran: Who will win the Hormuz endurance game?
stories.summary: Tehran is betting that its sanctions‑hardened economy can outlast the Strait of Hormuz crisis. Washington, meanwhile, is contending with elevated oil prices, renewed inflation pressure and rising global recession risks.

stories.title: Ukraine strike on Starobilsk dormitory kills four in occupied Luhansk
stories.summary: Ukrainian drone strike on a college dorm in Russian‑occupied Starobilsk killed four and wounded at least 35, while Russian drones also hit residential areas in Kherson.

stories.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trump's director of national intelligence
stories.summary: Tulsi Gabbard was appointed as director of national intelligence at the start of Trump's second term as US president.

stories.title: US government releases UFO sighting reports - 'Orbs swarming in all directions'
stories.summary: The Pentagon discloses scores of UFO files with reported sightings of spheres, discs and fireballs spanning nearly 80 years.

stories.title: Starmer says he will campaign for Burnham in Makerfield byelection – as it happened
stories.summary: Prime minister tells reporters he would campaign personally in the contest The Home Office has also published asylum figures this morning. These show that the number of asylum seekers being housed temporarily in hotels stood at 20,885 at the end of March 2026, down 35% year-on-year from 32,326. The Press Association says: It is the lowest figure since data was first reported in 2022, Home Office figures show. The total had climbed as high as 56,018 at the end of September 2023. Brits are leaving

stories.title: Rubio doubtful of diplomacy with Cuba as Trump renews threat of military action
stories.summary: US secretary of state says president would like a negotiated agreement with Havana but likelihood ‘is not high’ The US president, Donald Trump, and the secretary of state, Marco Rubio, on Thursday again raised the spectre of military intervention in Cuba, a renewed threat that takes on greater weight a day after the administration announced criminal charges against Raúl Castro, the island’s former leader. “Other presidents have looked at this for 50, 60 years, doing something,” Trump told report

stories.title: Venezuelan makeup artist who was deported to El Salvador seeks asylum in Spain: ‘I feel safe here’
stories.summary: Andry José Hernández Romero was deported from the US to El Salvador’s notorious Cecot prison before he was allowed to return to Venezuela One of the Venezuelan men sent from the US to El Salvador’s most notorious prison by Donald Trump has moved to Spain to request asylum after concluding that he did not feel safe back home and did not trust US authorities sufficiently to return to fight his legal case. Andry José Hernández Romero left Venezuela for Spain in early February and is due for his fir

stories.title: Hundreds protest over Ireland's 'George Floyd moment' after death of Congolese-born man – video
stories.summary: Hundreds of people protested outside Ireland's parliament on Thursday to express outrage at the death of a Congolese-born man after he was restrained outside a Dublin department store last week. Yves Sakila was detained by security guards on one of the capital's busiest shopping streets on 15 May in connection with an alleged shoplifting incident, police said. He became unresponsive at the scene and was later pronounced dead. A video of the incident widely shared days later on social media showe

stories.title: Turkey: DW's Alican Uludag released, but trial continues
stories.summary: A Turkish court has allowed DW reporter Alican Uludag to leave detention as his contentious trial opens. He is accused of having "insulted" President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, which in Turkey can carry a jail sentence.

stories.title: Israel says all Gaza flotilla activists have been deported amid global outrage over their treatment
stories.summary: Multiple European countries are calling for EU sanctions against Israeli national security minister Itamar Ben Gvir.

stories.title: Turkish court annuls leadership of main opposition CHP party, sparking turmoil
stories.summary: The case concerns allegations of vote buying at the CHP congress in November 2023, with prosecutors alleging that Özgür Özel secured his election as party leader with promises of jobs and other kickbacks.

stories.title: Rubio doubtful diplomacy would work with Cuban government as Trump raises threat of military action
stories.summary: Rubio says the White House is very focused on toppling the socialist government of Cuba to address what he says are national security risks. He also stressed that Havana will not be able to outwait the Trump administration, urging them to strike a deal before it is too late.

stories.title: Turkey welcomes 422 Gaza flotilla activists after Israel detention
stories.summary: 422 Gaza flotilla activists landed in Istanbul on Thursday after being deported by Israel. Several alleged physical abuse and torture following the interception of their aid convoy.

stories.title: Russian strike on Sumy injures six as mass drone attack hits Ukraine
stories.summary: Ukraine said six people, including a child, were injured after a Russian strike hit Sumy on Thursday. Firefighters spent hours tackling flames in residential and industrial areas damaged in the attack.

stories.title: Suspected Ebola cases triple in a week as WHO warns of rapid spread in DRC
stories.summary: Situation described as ‘deeply worrisome’ by officials as aid cuts and community distrust impede responders The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo poses a “very high” risk to the country, the World Health Organization said on Friday, revising its threat assessment upwards. The outbreak is spreading rapidly, WHO leaders said, with almost 750 suspected cases and 177 suspected deaths, up from 246 cases and 65 deaths when it was first reported a week earlier. Continue reading...

stories.title: Qatar sends mediators to Tehran in sign talks to reopen strait of Hormuz are reaching climax
stories.summary: Iran has threatened to impose tolls on shipping, while US demands that Iran hand over its highly enriched uranium may be deferred. Is there an end in sight? Qatar has rushed a team of mediators to Tehran in a sign that talks to open the strait of Hormuz, in return for the lifting of US sanctions and asset freezes, are reaching a climax. The aim would be to sign a memorandum of understanding on the strait that would lead to 30 days of talks on Iran’s nuclear programme – so deferring discussion of

stories.title: Can hybrid village stores answer rural Germany’s ‘cry for help’ and fend off far right?
stories.summary: Retail innovation, where people can shop alone after staff have gone, is helping vital community hubs survive Once upon a time, every German village had its own Tante Emma laden (Aunt Emma shop), a family-run hub of community life where local people bought their groceries at affordable prices and shot the breeze with their neighbours. But in recent years the loose network of small businesses throughout Europe’s biggest economy has come under huge pressure from staffing shortages, competition fro

stories.title: Pope decries ‘unscrupulous’ polluters at deadly, mafia-linked dumping ground
stories.summary: Pontiff visits ‘Land of Fires’ where dumping, burial and burning of waste has caused rise in cancer and other illnesses Pope Leo has met Italian families whose loved ones have died or have cancer as a result of illegal toxic dumping linked to a multi-billion-euro criminal racket run by the mafia. Leo’s visit to the Terra dei Fuochi , or Land of Fires, near Naples, came on the eve of the 11th anniversary of Pope Francis’ big ecological encyclical, Laudato Si (Praised Be), and indicates Leo’s comm

stories.title: Százezrek érkeztek a csíksomlyói búcsúra, Sulyok Tamás is ott volt
stories.summary: A Kárpát-medence legnagyobb római katolikus egyházi eseményének számító búcsú előtt a hatóságok pénteken három medvét hajtottak el a környékről.

stories.title: Franciaország kitiltotta a térdre kényszerített aktivistákat megalázó izraeli nemzetbiztonsági minisztert
stories.summary: A francia kormány EU-s szankciót szorgalmaz a szélsőjobboldali Itamar Ben-Gvirrel szemben.

stories.title: Nem szupervulkán, de aggasztó, hogy egyre inkább ébredezik
stories.summary: Egy új tanulmány szerint a Campi Flegrei aktivitása egyre gyorsuló tendenciát mutat. Ez komoly veszélyt jelent, egy évtizeden belül jelentős változás jöhet.

stories.title: Robbie Keane távozik a Ferencvárostól
stories.summary: A csapat közleménye szerint az edző döntött úgy, hogy elhagyja a csapatot.

stories.title: Moszkva szerint kollégiumba, Kijev szerint orosz parancsnoki állásba csapódtak az ukrán drónok
stories.summary: Oroszország szerint 16 halottja van a pénteki ukrán akciónak az oroszok által megszállt Luhanszk megyében.

stories.title: A BL-döntőn és egy rakás állami rendezvényen dolgozó kisebb cég került bajba, miután Balásyék számláit zárolták
stories.summary: A tényleges munkát végző alvállalkozók most nem kapják meg a pénzüket. Sürgős megoldásért kiált a helyzet, de egyelőre azt sem tudni, kit kellene keresni a kormánynál az ügyben.

stories.title: Azbesztet találtak több Nagy-Britanniában árult gyerekjátékban
stories.summary: Egy játékbolt-tulajdonos szerint nincs előírva független ellenőrzés, mielőtt a termékek piacra kerülnének, és sok brit kereskedő csak a sajtóból értesült arról, hogy egyes játékokat már máshol visszahívtak.

stories.title: Vizsgálóbizottságot állított fel a Mol a tiszaújvárosi robbanás után
stories.summary: Megkezdődött az érintett üzemegység biztonságos leürítése, ezt követően indulhatnak el a részletes kárfelmérési munkálatok.

stories.title: Russell rajt-cél győzelmet aratott a Forma-1 sprintfutamon
stories.summary: Russell mögött a vb-címvédő Lando Norris, a McLaren brit pilótája ért célba másodikként, míg a harmadik helyen Kimi Antonelli zárt.

stories.title: Vancsa Zalán gólja döntött a belga osztályozón, feljutott az élvonalba a csapata
stories.summary: A Lommel jövőre a legjobbak közt bizonyíthat.

stories.title: Soha nem látott májusi hőség szakadt Franciaországra
stories.summary: Az egész országban 10-15 fokkal magasabb a hőmérséklet a májusban megszokottnál, ami nem egyszeri eseménynek tűnik, hanem a klímaváltozás újabb jele lehet.

stories.title: Amikor őrizetbe vették a reptéren, bevallotta, hogy szökésben van. Aztán húzott egy váratlant
stories.summary: Lee Gilley-t 2024-ben vádolták meg azzal, hogy megölte a várandós feleségét. Texas államban ezért akár halálbüntetést is kiszabhatnak rá. Május végén kezdődött volna a tárgyalása, de a férfi meglépett. Rejtélyes úton eljutott Olaszországba, amivel legalábbis a halálbüntetés kiszabását megakadályozná.

stories.title: Nem sokon múlt a meglepetés a Kanadai Nagydíj időmérőjén
stories.summary: Az időjárás eső nélkül is megkavarta egy kicsit a mezőnyt, a második szakaszt egyik Mercedes sem zárta dobogós helyen.

stories.title: The dying dream of the dead to be at peace with life
stories.summary: Many people have vivid dreams shortly before death. Research suggests the dreams are not a sign of confusion — but may help both the dying and their loved ones make sense of loss.

stories.title: Kevin Warsh: Trump ally at the Federal Reserve?
stories.summary: Never before has a chair of the US Federal Reserve been so controversial. Who is Kevin Warsh? Can he preserve the Fed's independence, or will he become Donald Trump's puppet?

stories.title: Germany's 'rapid militarization' threatens basic rights
stories.summary: Ten human rights organizations say security interests are increasingly being prioritized over fundamental rights in Germany. The scathing criticism was presented in their "2026 Fundamental Rights Report."

stories.title: What is Pentecost?
stories.summary: People new to Germany are often surprised by the number of public holidays in the spring. Pentecost is one of them, but its significance is not well-known.

stories.title: Lebanon opens seafront tent camp in Beirut as Israeli strikes displace 1 million
stories.summary: Lebanon has opened a large tent camp on the seafront in Beirut to shelter civilians displaced by Israeli bombardment and fighting linked to Hezbollah, as more than one million people have now been forced from their homes.

stories.title: USA: MomoCon in Atlanta unites 65,000 fans for a huge geek culture fest
stories.summary: MomoCon opened this week in Atlanta, with organisers expecting around 65,000 gamers, anime fans and cosplayers to attend one of the country’s biggest celebrations of geek culture, providing a major boost to local businesses.

stories.title: Bigger than ever: WUF13 ends with global call for action
stories.summary: As the World Urban Forum ended in Baku on Friday, the debate on the future of cities evolved beyond architecture, infrastructure and skylines to the urgent global question of how can cities withstand conflict, climate change, rapid urbanisation and inequality without leaving communities behind.

stories.title: Trump is putting pressure on Cuba - why and to what end?
stories.summary: The US has warned that a peaceful agreement with Cuba is unlikely, Havana insists this is a "fraudulent case" designed to justify military intervention.

stories.title: UAE-backed programme broadens assessment of public institutions
stories.summary: Joint Uzbek and Emirati evaluation teams reviewed ministries and agencies using criteria including transparency, digitalisation and service delivery. Officials say future assessments will place greater emphasis on measurable results.

stories.title: Tulsi Gabbard to resign as US national intelligence director
stories.summary: Gabbard who has been largely out of public view during recent US operations says she is leaving due to her husband's illness.

stories.title: 'Speed, money and compassion' - lessons from an Ebola survivor and other experts
stories.summary: Those caught up in West Africa's Ebola outbreak a decade ago on how best to tackle the current epidemic.

stories.title: Race for French presidency sees ex-PM Philippe as early favourite to beat populists
stories.summary: Latest polls suggest the centre-right figure is the only candidate who can defeat Marine Le Pen or Jean-Luc Mélenchon.

stories.title: The space race to create gym equipment for future astronauts
stories.summary: Scientists are attempting to build exercise equipment to be used on future space flights.

stories.title: Moment SpaceX rocket explodes in the Indian Ocean after splashdown
stories.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX has successfully launched its Starship V3 rocket, which landed in a planned fiery explosion on Friday.

stories.title: SpaceX launches massive Starship V3 rocket on test flight
stories.summary: The largest and most powerful rocket in history blasted off after its first attempted launch was postponed.

stories.title: In the race to succeed Trump, Vance and Rubio set for unpopularity contest among Republicans
stories.summary: When Republicans choose Donald Donald Trump’s successor next year, they will also be deciding the future of the populist MAGA movement. So far, Trump is keeping his strategy close to his chest.

stories.title: WHO says Ebola risk now 'very high' in DR Congo
stories.summary: WHO head Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said on Friday that the global risk from the disease remained low.

stories.title: Xi Jinping urges 'all-out rescue' after coal mine explosion kills at least 82 in China
stories.summary: Six emergency rescue teams consisting of 345 personnel have since been sent to the scene, the Xinhua state news agency reported on Saturday.

stories.title: Gaza flotilla activists allege abuse by Israeli forces while detained
stories.summary: The detainees say they were slapped, hit, humiliated and even subjected to sexual violence, but Israel's prison service says the allegations are false.

stories.title: DR Congo players told to isolate before World Cup
stories.summary: The Democratic Republic of Congo's World Cup squad must isolate for 21 days before they can enter the US because of the Ebola outbreak.

stories.title: US says people seeking green cards must leave the country to apply
stories.summary: Applying for a green card can take months or longer, meaning people could be forced to leave their families or jobs whilst the process is ongoing.

stories.title: Senegal's president sacks PM and former ally after months-long feud
stories.summary: Bassirou Diomaye Faye also dissolved the government, creating uncertainty amid a debt crisis in the country.

stories.title: Rubio meets Modi during India visit with energy high on agenda
stories.summary: The secretary of state wants to sell US energy to Delhi to make up for shortfalls caused by the Iran war.

stories.title: Parody Cockroach Janta political party’s rise reflects youth anger in India
stories.summary: Satirical project is viral sensation and outlet for protest on social media as it taps into young people’s frustration It began as a satirical online project after India’s chief justice compared unemployed young people to cockroaches. Now millions of young Indians are flocking to it as an outlet for their frustration. A parody political party with the insect as its symbol has exploded across India’s social media by turning absurdist humour into protest. Memes and short videos mocking corruption,

stories.title: US arms sales to Taiwan on ‘pause’ due to Iran war, says acting navy chief
stories.summary: Hung Cao’s comments are latest blow to Taipei after Donald Trump recently cast doubt over US’s enduring support US arms sales to Taiwan have been “paused” to ensure the US military has enough munitions for its Iran operations, according to Washington’s acting navy secretary, in the latest blow to Taipei after a series of comments by Donald Trump. When asked at a congressional hearing on Thursday about a $14bn (£10.4bn) weapons package awaiting Trump’s signoff for months, Hung Cao said: “Right no

stories.title: Electoral reform and reversing Brexit: they’re more connected than you might think | Tom Baldwin
stories.summary: Labour’s emerging leadership contest is reopening the EU debate. But if we want to rejoin, Britain needs a more European voting system first Nowhere is an anniversary more relished than in newspapers. As we approach the 10-year mark since Britain voted for Brexit, countless column inches would no doubt have been reserved for this purpose anyway. Yet the prospect of a Labour leadership contest, at a time when polls are showing four-fifths of the party’s voters at the last election and an even hig

stories.title: What happened to jailed Belarus dissident Raman Pratasevich?
stories.summary: In 2021, Belarus diverted a Ryanair plane to seize opposition journalist Raman Pratasevich. What has happened to him since, and why is he now seemingly supporting the Belarusian regime?

stories.title: Cities must plan for migration as permanent reality, experts say at World Urban Forum
stories.summary: Governments must treat migration as a permanent feature of urban planning, not an emergency, the IOM deputy director general told Euronews at the World Urban Forum in Baku.

stories.title: 'No means no': Greenlanders protest against Trump outside new US consulate
stories.summary: The inauguration of the new consulate comes amid a push by the US president for greater control over the island.

stories.title: Erdoğan signs decree closing Istanbul's Bilgi University amid criminal probe into owner
stories.summary: As part of an investigation into Can Holding, the operating licence of Istanbul Bilgi University was revoked after its founding foundation was placed under trusteeship. The decision has raised concerns about the future of its students and academic staff.

stories.title: Greece protesters marched in Athens outside Israeli embassy
stories.summary: Around 1,000 pro-Palestinian demonstrators marched through Athens on Thursday after Israël intercepted a flotilla carrying Gaza activists, gathering outside the Israeli embassy as international criticism over the arrests mounted.

stories.title: Taliban ‘legitimising child marriage’ with new law, activists warn
stories.summary: Up to 70% of girls may be in early or forced marriages but law now makes divorce impossible if husbands disagree Child marriage appears to have been legally recognised for the first time by the Taliban in Afghanistan, as activists say “shameful” new laws make it almost impossible for girls and young women to seek divorce against their husbands’ will. There are no official statistics on forced and underage marriages in Afghanistan, but activists say it has risen at an alarming rate in recent year

stories.title: Weather tracker: Heavy rain triggers flooding and landslides in parts of China
stories.summary: Twelve dead as southern and central areas also suffer travel disruption, electricity outages and evacuations Heavy, prolonged rainfall in southern and central China has resulted in weather warnings for flash flooding, landslides and waterlogging. The slow-moving band of rainfall spanned 620 miles (1,000km) and travelled eastwards across the regions on Tuesday. It was formed from the convergence of multiple bands of rain originating from the Bay of Bengal, South China Sea and Pacific Ocean. Given

stories.title: UN’s climate crisis vote shows political momentum is growing, say experts
stories.summary: Resolution backed by 141 states hailed as ‘new chapter’ that could improve climate diplomacy and litigation efforts When the UN general assembly voted overwhelmingly in favour of a landmark climate crisis ruling on Wednesday, the Pacific island of Vanuatu’s prime minister hailed the result as the start of “a new chapter” in climate action. “The task before all of us now is to translate legal clarity into meaningful action, stronger cooperation, and greater protection for present and future gener
